weekNoteHtml:
How to use this page. Three movement patterns per competition event. Pick the events you want to train, then do all three patterns for that event — or cherry-pick across events. Aim for 4–6 patterns total per session. Warm up first; stop a set short if form breaks down.

Tap any movement's teal header to open it full-screen — easy to read on the gym iPad, pinch to zoom, scroll through all three exercises.

This week — all-new movements. Power emphasis: speed-strength and rate of force — lighter and faster than last block's grind, moving at competition speed. Everything is fresh this week; read the cues, chase bar and ball speed, and stop at 3 sets. The moment reps slow down, you're done — never add a set.
testsLineHtml: Working number week. No formal test this week — tap your name below anytime to log a working number you want to track.

patterns.name: Box Squat @ % of Max Reps
patterns.rx: 3 sets · each set to ~60% of your best box-squat AMRAP · rest 2 min between sets
patterns.load: Your competition KB
cues: The app shows your personal rep target — hit that number cleanly across all 3 sets and stop there.
Sit fully to the box and stand tall on every rep; the box keeps your depth honest.
Keep the KB tight to your chest with your heels down. Stop early if your form breaks.

patterns.name: KB Swing to Box Squat
patterns.rx: 3 × 6 reps · rest 90 sec between sets · one hard swing, then one box squat = 1 rep
patterns.load: A KB about 1 size lighter than competition for crisp swings
cues: Snap your hips to float the bell to chest height — let the hips do the work.
Catch it at your chest and flow straight into a fast box squat.
Keep your back flat on the swing and your arms relaxed.
Stop if the swing gets sloppy — every rep should be sharp.

patterns.name: Speed Box Squat to Calf Raise
patterns.rx: 3 × 10 reps · rest 75 sec between sets · fast up off the box, then rise onto your toes
patterns.load: A KB about 1-2 sizes lighter than your comp KB, light enough to move quickly
cues: Stand fast off the box and finish by pressing up onto your toes.
Lower your heels under control before the next squat.
Keep the whole rep quick and bouncy without losing balance.
Hold a wall or rack with one hand if the toe-raise feels tippy.

patterns.name: Throw Technique Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 4 throws · rest 90 sec between sets · 12 fast throws, rehearse comp-day speed
patterns.load: Dynamax ball — 4 lb (women) / 8 lb (men)
cues: Hinge and explode up, throwing the ball up and back over your head — make the ball move as fast as possible.
This week chase speed: a quick, snappy hip drive on every throw rather than a slow grind.
Keep the arms loose and fast so the ball whips overhead; let the hips fire first.
Reset your stance crisply between throws and keep your stomach braced through the finish.

patterns.name: KB Swing — Light and Fast
patterns.rx: 3 × 10 reps · rest 75 sec between sets
patterns.load: A KB you can float to chest height fast, about 1 size below your comp swing bell — roughly 10-15 lb lighter so it flies up weightless
cues: Hike the bell back between your legs, then snap your hips forward fast to float it to chest or eye height.
Chase speed, not grind — a sharp, quick hip snap on every rep so the bell flies up weightless.
Let the bell swing down on its own and load the next rep; don't muscle it with your arms or shoulders.
Brace your stomach at the top so your lower back stays flat, ribs over hips.

patterns.name: Vertical Jump — Countermovement
patterns.rx: 3 × 4 reps · rest 90 sec between sets
patterns.load: Bodyweight, jumping straight up for height on a flat, non-slip surface
cues: Dip quickly into a quarter squat, then jump straight up as high as you can, reaching tall with both arms.
Make the dip-and-jump one fast, snappy motion — this rehearses the explosive pop your throw needs.
Land soft on both feet with bent knees, absorbing quietly, then reset before the next jump.
Hold a sturdy support nearby for balance if you need it, and stop if landing feels shaky.

patterns.name: Bench Volume @ % of Max Reps
patterns.rx: 3 sets · each set to ~45% of your max-rep test · rest 2 min between · max bar speed every rep
patterns.load: Your competition bench weight
cues: Your app shows your personal rep target — keep the reps few and fast, exploding the bar off your chest every time.
This week is about speed: lighten the effort by stopping well short of a grind and chasing bar velocity instead.
End the set as soon as the bar slows down even slightly or your hips lift off the bench.

patterns.name: Speed Push-Up
patterns.rx: 3 × 6 reps · rest 90 sec between sets · pop off the floor
patterns.load: Bodyweight (hands on a bench to scale easier)
cues: Lower under control, then press up explosively so your hands leave the floor a touch at the top.
Land soft with slightly bent elbows and reset your body in a straight line before the next rep.
Scale easier by placing your hands on a bench. Stop the set when you can't get any pop off the floor.

patterns.name: Overhead D-Ball Slam to Press Position
patterns.rx: 3 × 8 reps · rest 90 sec between sets · fast, snappy reps
patterns.load: A light D-ball you can move fast for 8 reps
cues: Hold a light D-ball at your chest and punch it straight overhead as fast as you can, then bring it back down under control.
Punch the ball overhead fast and lock the arms out tall, then lower it under control to your chest before the next rep.
Keep your stomach braced so you don't arch your lower back. Stop if the speed drops off.

patterns.name: Max Hang Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × hold for time · rest 2 min 30 sec between
patterns.load: Pull-up bar at ~80% of your current best hang time — crisp setups, slightly lighter feel so you stay fresh, step down at 80%
cues: Jump up, settle into a packed-shoulder hang fast, and hold clean.
Grip hard with spread fingers and keep your breathing slow.
Step down with control rather than dropping when grip fades.
Record the hold time for the app.

patterns.name: Explosive Band Pulldown
patterns.rx: 3 × 12 reps · rest 75 sec between sets
patterns.load: A resistance band anchored overhead — pick a band tension you can pull fast for a snappy 12
cues: Stand or kneel under the anchor and pull the band to your hips fast and hard.
Drive the elbows down with speed, then control the band back up slowly.
Keep your chest up and shoulder blades working on every quick rep.
Move the band fast — this is about speed and rate of force, not grinding.

patterns.name: Fat-Grip Bar Hang
patterns.rx: 3 × hold for time · rest 90 sec between
patterns.load: Fat-grip attachments clamped on the pull-up bar, one hand each · pick a hold goal of 15–25 seconds
cues: Wrap your hands around the fat grips and crush hard — the thick surface forces an explosive grip.
Pack the shoulders and keep your body still so you do not start to swing.
Step down safely the instant your hand starts to open.
Spread your fingers around the fat grip for the strongest possible hold.

patterns.name: Standing Slam Volume
patterns.rx: 3 × 8 reps · rest 90 sec between sets
patterns.load: Your competition D-ball
cues: Use your competition D-ball so the slam moves fast — speed is the whole point.
Snap each slam down as quickly as you can while keeping full overhead reach.
If reps slow down, rest a little longer rather than grinding.

patterns.name: Pogo Hops
patterns.rx: 3 × 20 sec · rest 90 sec between sets
patterns.load: Bodyweight
cues: Bounce quickly off the balls of your feet with stiff ankles, like a quick spring.
Stay tall and keep ground contacts short and snappy — minimal knee bend.
Keep your legs nearly straight and rebound the instant your feet touch the floor.

patterns.name: Banded Overhead Slam
patterns.rx: 3 × 8 reps · rest 90 sec between sets
patterns.load: A light band anchored overhead plus a light D-ball, 8 lb (women) / 15 lb (men)
cues: With a band adding downward pull, slam the light ball down as fast as you possibly can.
Chase bar speed — the band trains your arms to accelerate all the way to the floor.
Keep a full reach overhead before each fast slam.

patterns.name: 60-Sec Pace Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 60 sec of singles · rest 2 min between sets · race-speed turnover
patterns.load: Your jump rope, rehearsing race-day speed
cues: Rehearse the exact fast pace you want on comp day and feel it become automatic.
Keep your feet quick and your bounce tiny so the rope never catches you.
Make each set crisp and fast, then rest fully before the next.

patterns.name: Rebound Calf Raise
patterns.rx: 3 × 15 reps · rest 90 sec between sets · fast up, controlled down
patterns.load: A DB/KB you can move fast for 15 reps, roughly 10-20 lb per hand
cues: Snap up onto the balls of your feet as quickly as possible on each rep.
Keep the load light so speed never slows down across the set.
Lower under control and reset your stretch before the next fast rep.

patterns.name: Quick-Contact Skips
patterns.rx: 3 × 20 sec of fast skips · rest 90 sec between sets
patterns.load: Bodyweight, fast light skipping in place
cues: Skip in place with the lightest, fastest ground contacts you can manage.
Stay tall on the balls of your feet and barely touch the floor.
Keep it snappy and relaxed rather than forceful and heavy.

patterns.name: Broad Jump — Distance Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 4 jumps · rest 2 min between sets · lighter intent, fast and sharp
patterns.load: Bodyweight
cues: Focus on speed off the floor — quick arm swing, fast hip snap, jump forward and stick both feet.
Rehearse competition-pattern velocity: crisp and explosive rather than grinding for every last inch.
Reset fully between reps. Every jump should feel snappy; stop if they start to drag.

patterns.name: Continuous Low Hurdle Hops
patterns.rx: 3 × 8 hops · rest 90 sec between sets · fast off the floor
patterns.load: Bodyweight over a line of low, ankle-height objects (rolled towels or small cones)
cues: Hop over each low object with both feet, spending as little time on the ground as possible between hops.
Stay tall on the balls of your feet and let your ankles spring — small, quick, light contacts.
Keep the objects ankle-height and stop the set if your hops get heavy or your landings get flat.

patterns.name: Banded Hip-Snap Pull-Through
patterns.rx: 3 × 12 reps · rest 75 sec between sets · snap fast, return slow
patterns.load: A resistance band anchored low behind you, tension you can snap through for a sharp 12 reps
cues: Hinge your hips back to load the band, then snap your hips forward fast to stand tall and squeeze your glutes.
Drive the movement with a quick hip snap, not your arms or lower back — let the band ride between your legs.
Control the band on the way back to reset each rep. Keep your spine flat throughout.

patterns.name: 500m Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 200m · rest 2 min fully between · sprint pace, faster than race
patterns.load: Rower · about 4–5 sec/500m FASTER than your goal 500m split — short and quick
cues: Short sprints — chase a fast split and keep the stroke rate up around 30+.
Snap the legs down and rip the handle in, then recover smooth and reset for the next stroke.
Quality over grinding — if the split slows, stop the piece and take your rest.

patterns.name: Band-Resisted Row Pull
patterns.rx: 3 × 10 fast reps · rest 75 sec between sets
patterns.load: A resistance band light enough to snap back fast for all 10 reps
cues: Anchor the band ahead of you, sit tall, and pull the handles to your ribs as fast as you can.
Let the band pull your arms back out under control — don't let it yank you forward.
Every rep is a quick, sharp pull; the band should move fast, not slow.

patterns.name: Speed Lat Pulldown
patterns.rx: 3 × 8 fast reps · rest 75 sec between sets
patterns.load: A cable stack light enough to pull down explosively for all 8 reps
cues: Grab the bar, sit tall, and pull it down to your collarbone as fast as you can.
Control the bar all the way back up — the speed is on the pull-down, not the return.
Keep your chest up and don't lean way back; let the lats snap the bar down.

patterns.name: Shuttle Pace Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 100-yd shuttle (2 × 50 yd) · rest 2–3 min · top-speed lengths
patterns.load: Bodyweight · run these at near-top speed to rehearse race velocity
cues: Run the two 50s fast, focusing on quick leg turnover and a relaxed upper body.
Decelerate under control into the turn, plant, and snap back out — fast feet, smooth plant.
Full rest keeps every rep at true speed across all three sets.

patterns.name: Jump Squat
patterns.rx: 3 × 5 reps · fast off the floor · rest 2 min
patterns.load: Bodyweight, or light dumbbells ~10–15 lb each — light enough to jump fast
cues: Dip a quarter of the way down, then jump as high and fast as you can, landing soft.
Move the load with speed — lighten or drop the weights if they slow you down at all.
Rate of force is the goal; reset between every fast, explosive rep.

patterns.name: Build-Up Strides
patterns.rx: 3 × 30 yards · rest 75 sec between sets · accelerate smoothly to ~80%, never all-out
patterns.load: Bodyweight
cues: Start at a jog and build speed over 30 yards to about 80% — fast and smooth, not a flat-out sprint.
Ease off gradually at the end instead of slamming the brakes.
Walk back and recover fully between reps. If anything feels tight, stop for the day.

patterns.name: Comp-Distance Sled Push
patterns.rx: 3 × 50 yards · rest 2.5 min between · fast and crisp (not timed)
patterns.load: Competition load: 2 × 25 lb plates (women) / 2 × 45 lb plates (men) · comp weight for speed
cues: Push at competition weight (2x25 lb women / 2x45 lb men), fast and crisp, not as a timed test.
Stay at about 45° with arms locked and move your feet quick — push through your trunk, never bending the elbows.
Drive fast, long steps and rest fully so every push is sharp and explosive.

patterns.name: Sprint Build-Up
patterns.rx: 3 × 30 yards · rest 2 min between · accelerate to fast, not a max test
patterns.load: Bodyweight only, building speed smoothly over the 30 yards
cues: Start easy and smoothly build up to a fast, controlled run over the 30 yards to rehearse the leg turnover of the push.
Stay tall and relaxed, pumping your arms — don't strain or lunge for top speed.
Walk back and recover fully between runs so each build-up feels smooth and quick, not labored.

patterns.name: Banded Speed Squat
patterns.rx: 3 × 5 reps · rest 2 min between · explosive up out of the bottom
patterns.load: A light barbell or empty bar — light enough that every rep moves fast and snappy
cues: Sit down under control then stand up as fast as you can to train the speed-strength your push needs.
Keep the load light so the bar moves quickly — this is about speed, not grinding a heavy weight.
Stay tall with your chest up, and reset your stance before each rep so every one is a fast, clean effort.
